text: I came down quite suddenly with a high fever, muscle aches all over, and a persistent dry cough. 1 day(s) and barely functioning, and I can't seem to shake it, which frightens me.
label: influenza/mild

text: I thought I'd stop in — not quite myself lately, probably nothing.
label: non-infectious

text: Cough with some yellowish mucus and a dull chest ache for 3 day(s), and I feel more unwell than yesterday.
label: influenza/mild

text: Slight fever, some body aches, scratchy throat. Probably flu starting, 3 day(s), though I notice it's not improving. I can't catch my breath.
label: influenza/mild

text: I can barely move — high fever, shaking chills, splitting headache, bad cough. 4 day(s) like this, and it's escalating — I'm genuinely scared. I have a very high fever.
label: influenza/moderate

text: Productive cough and slightly short of breath climbing stairs. 4 day(s) like this. Breathing is very laboured.
label: pneumonia/mild

text: Slight fever, some body aches, scratchy throat. Probably flu starting, 4 day(s), and I'm getting worse, not better. I think my oxygen might be low.
label: pneumonia/mild

text: High fever, bad body aches, shivering, exhausted. Classic flu, but quite rough — 1 day(s), and nothing is improving — I'm very worried.
label: influenza/mild

text: I'm mostly fine, just wanted to rule anything out.
label: non-infectious

text: Gasping with each breath, sharp chest pain, high fever, thick productive cough. 9 day(s) now, though I think I'm slowly getting better.
label: pneumonia/severe

text: I've had a low-grade fever and achy muscles for 9 day(s) — nothing terrible yet, though it seems to be slowly resolving. I have a very high fever.
label: influenza/mild

text: Slight fever, some body aches, scratchy throat. Probably flu starting, 3 day(s).
label: influenza/mild

text: I came down quite suddenly with a high fever, muscle aches all over, and a persistent dry cough. 8 day(s) and barely functioning, and it feels like it might be turning a corner.
label: influenza/moderate

text: Mild fatigue, thought it worth a quick check.
label: non-infectious
